message:
feat(atc): integrate ATC engine into server runtime with 6 alien-artifact tracks

Wire the previously-dormant AtcEngine (5,167-line expected-loss decision
theory library) into the live server, and add six new mathematically
principled enhancement tracks that close the gap between "well-tested
library" and "fully self-tuning coordination engine."

## Server integration (lib.rs)

- Call atc::init_global_atc(config) in both run_stdio() and run_http()
  startup paths, creating a process-wide AtcEngine singleton via
  OnceLock<Mutex<AtcEngine>>
- Hook every tool dispatch to call atc::atc_observe_activity(agent, ts)
  after successful execution, feeding agent liveness tracking
- Detect register_agent / macro_start_session completions and call
  atc::atc_register_agent() to enroll new agents in ATC monitoring

## Alien-artifact enhancement tracks (atc.rs, +1034 lines)

Track 16 — Regret → PID → Loss Matrix Feedback Loop:
  LossMatrixTuner<A,S> maintains a PID controller per (action, state)
  pair. Per-pair regret accumulated from atc_record_outcome() is fed
  as error signal every 50 decisions, auto-tuning the DecisionCore's
  asymmetric loss matrices online. Safety bounds prevent degenerate
  matrices via PidState min/max clamping.

Track 17 — Conformal Prediction Sets:
  AtcConformalSet with per-subsystem SubsystemConformal trackers.
  Records nonconformity scores (|predicted - actual| loss), computes
  calibrated prediction interval widths at 90% coverage. High
  uncertainty gates aggressive actions — the engine suppresses
  ReleaseReservations when the liveness subsystem's conformal interval
  is wide, preventing premature reservation cleanup.

Track 18 — Hierarchical Bayesian Agent Population Model:
  HierarchicalAgentModel maintains per-program-type PopulationStats
  (mean, variance, pseudo-count) via Welford-style online updates.
  New agents inherit population-informed priors rather than hardcoded
  constants, and every 50 ticks the population model is refreshed
  from observed agent rhythms (empirical Bayes).

Track 19 — Contextual Bandits for Adaptive Thresholds:
  AdaptiveThreshold per agent using a Beta(α,β) posterior over the
  true-positive rate of suspicion decisions. effective_k() maps the
  posterior mean to a suspicion k-factor: high TP → lower k (more
  aggressive detection), high FP → higher k (more conservative).
  Bounded to [1.5, 5.0] to prevent extremes.

Track 20 — Submodular Probe Scheduling:
  submodular_probe_schedule() implements lazy greedy submodular
  maximization combining entropy-based information gain with recency
  decay (recently-probed agents get penalized). Diminishing returns
  model with cardinality constraint (max 3 probes per tick) gives a
  (1-1/e)-approximation to optimal.

Track 21 — Survival Analysis for Agent Liveness:
  KaplanMeierEstimator tracks empirical survival function S(t) for
  agent silence durations with right-censoring support. hazard_rate()
  computes instantaneous failure rate. Fed from atc_record_outcome()
  with observed silence durations and death/alive ground truth.

## Global API surface

- init_global_atc(config) — one-time initialization
- atc_enabled() — check if ATC is live
- atc_register_agent(name, program) — enroll agent + adaptive threshold
- atc_observe_activity(agent, ts) — liveness heartbeat
- atc_tick(now) → Vec<AtcTickAction> — periodic evaluation returning
  SendAdvisory / ReleaseReservations / ProbeAgent actions
- atc_record_outcome(subsystem, agent, predicted, actual, correct) —
  calibration feedback feeding all 6 enhancement tracks
- atc_summary() → AtcSummarySnapshot — robot mode / TUI display

## Lock ordering discipline

All global statics use separate OnceLock<Mutex<T>>. Lock ordering is
enforced by convention: ENGINE is never held when acquiring TUNER,
SURVIVAL, THRESHOLDS, or CONFORMAL. The survival estimator section
extracts data from ENGINE first (acquire+release) before separately
locking SURVIVAL, avoiding nested locks.

## Test coverage

23 new tests covering all 6 tracks plus global integration. Combined
with existing tests: 170 ATC tests pass, 0 failures. Clean clippy
with -D warnings, clean cargo fmt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
